frameworks.name: latent cause inference
frameworks.one_line: experience is segmented into inferred latent causes supporting context-dependent learning

frameworks.name: Bayesian nonparametric inference
frameworks.one_line: controls a context module that assigns a unique context vector per inferred latent cause

frameworks.name: connectionist neural network
frameworks.one_line: LCNet stores shared structure across tasks in network weights

frameworks.name: schema learning
frameworks.one_line: curriculum effects in acquiring shared structure across contexts

core_question: How can splitting experience into latent causes and learning shared structure across contexts be achieved simultaneously?
key_assumptions: experience is parsed into discrete latent causes
shared structure across contexts can be stored in shared network weights
context-specific structure is captured via per-LC context vectors
Bayesian nonparametric prior governs assignment of new latent causes
manipulations: task structure with shared versus context-specific components
stimulus stream as naturalistic videos of daily events
key_findings: LCNet extracts shared structure across latent causes in function learning while avoiding catastrophic interference
LCNet captures human curriculum effects in schema learning
LCNet infers underlying event structure from naturalistic daily-event videos
interpretations: a context module gated by Bayesian nonparametric inference reconciles shared and context-specific learning
neural network models can implement latent-cause inference while preserving generalization across contexts
